Authentication HackerRank uses OAuth 2.0 for authentication. All API requests must include a Bearer token in the Authorization header. Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN To obtain an access token: - Register at https://www.hackerrank.com/developers - Create an application - Use the provided client_id and client_secret in OAuth flow 2. User Management Endpoints GET /api/v1/users/{user_id} Returns user profile information including name, email, username, reputation score, and account status. Parameters: user_id (required) Response: User object with fields: id, username, email, created_at, reputation, badges POST /api/v1/users/{user_id}/profile Update user profile information. Parameters: name, bio, avatar_url (optional) Response: Updated user object GET /api/v1/users/{user_id}/submissions Lists all submissions made by the user. Source: api_documentation.txt;troubleshooting_guide.txt.

HackerRank Troubleshooting Guide SUBMISSION FAILURES Problem: Code compiles locally but fails on HackerRank Causes: - Different environment/library versions - Incorrect input/output format - Unhandled edge cases - Platform-specific behavior Solution Steps: 1. Review test case examples carefully 2. Check input constraints and data ranges 3. Test with maximum constraint values 4. Verify output format exactly (spaces, newlines, precision) 5. Check for integer overflow in calculations 6. Source: troubleshooting_guide.txt;api_documentation.txt.

System Prompts Use system prompts to define Claude's behavior: - Describe the role or persona - Set guidelines for responses - Define output format - Specify tone and style Example: "You are a helpful support agent. Keep responses concise (under 100 words). Always be professional and empathetic. Provide specific solutions when possible." 3. Handling Long Contexts For documents over 20k tokens: - Break into sections - Prioritize relevant sections first - Use summaries of less important parts - Reference specific sections by title 4. Cost Optimization - Use Haiku for simple tasks to save costs - Cache repeated requests where possible - Batch similar requests together - Monitor token usage with logging 6.
